data.prompt_used:
You are a product catalog quality reviewer. You will analyze a product listing across image, text fields, and a category mapping, and return a strict JSON verdict for each defined task 

Two images are attached to this request, in this exact order:
  1) IMAGE 1 - Product Photo. The actual product being listed.
  2) IMAGE 2 - Category Photo. A generic reference showing the type of product the category sells.
Do not confuse the two images. 

Isolation rule for task 1 and task 2.
For Task 1 (Primary Object) and Task 2 (OCR), use ONLY the Product Photo above.
Do NOT read, peek at, or use any of the Text Inputs declared further below in this prompt 
 
Instructions:
Task 1:
  Image Analysis: Carefully Detect the primary object from IMAGE 1 (Product Photo). 
  Avoid generic terms, unless no specific object can be inferred. Do not invent features not visible.

Task 2:
  - OCR (Optical Character Recognition): Carefully Extract text from the IMAGE 1 (Product Photo). 
  - Include only unique, clearly readable, product-related text (deduplicate repeated words/logos).
  - Exclude watermarks, overlays, background patterns, decorative text, reflections, serials on wires/cables, partial or uncertain characters, repeated branding, and noise.
  - Strictly base all OCR on the visible text and components in the provided image. Do not infer, normalize, correct, or hallucinate text. Preserve visible casing and spelling.
  - If the image is noisy, include only confidently readable text.
  - Max length: 200 characters.
  - Do NOT read text from IMAGE 2 (attached to this request)

Product Title: Child and Baby Care Products
Product Specifications: Quantity: 24 Pack, Packaging Type: Plastic Lid Pack, Material: Non Woven, Probable Order Value: Rs. 1,100 - 1,300
Category Name: Baby Wet Wipe
Category Photo: provided as IMAGE 2 

Category Photo IMAGE 2 Disclaimer: The Category Photo IMAGE 2 is a generic reference showing the type of product sold in this category — not a visual standard. Products may differ in  color, shape, size, design, or model and still belong to the category. Use it only to understand the product class (e.g., "this category sells pumps"). 

Treat the Task 1 and Task 2 answers as fixed inputs . Do NOT revise them at this stage even if the text inputs suggest otherwise. 

Task 3:
Product Search Query : Generate a query using Primary Object (from Task 1) + OCR Extracted text (from Task 2) + Product Title + Product Specifications to represent the product.
 - Maximum word limit: 5.
 - Query should be relevant, grammatically correct, SEO-friendly for ecommerce search and should be able to represent the product. 
   Example: 1) bosch gsb550 power drill. 
            2) 5 ton hydraulic floor jack
            3) 200 litre stainless storage tank
            4) red cordless handheld vacuum
 - Recheck the query to make sure that it is meaningful.

Task 4: Contradiction Check
Identify if there is contradiction in each pair below.
Analyze each pair independently. An "outlier" here requires a mismatch between the two listed sources. If the sources agree on the core fact, it is "not outlier," even if one source is poorly formatted or has internal errors.
If the product title describes a service (e.g., 'Printing Services', 'Repair Service', 'Installation Service') and the photo shows the physical subject or substrate of that service, this is NOT a contradiction. Such photos are standard practice for service listings.
  4a) Primary Object of IMAGE 1 (Product Photo) - Product Title
   Only flag as outlier when the photo shows a fundamentally different product type - e.g., photo shows a motorcycle when title says 'bicycle', or photo shows a ceiling fan when title says 'table fan'. Specificity gaps (photo more specific than title) are never contradictions - e.g., a red cordless handheld vacuum cleaner photographed under the title 'Vacuum Cleaner' is not a contradiction.
  4b) IMAGE 1 (Product Photo)  -  Product Title
  4c) Primary Object of IMAGE 1 (Product Photo) -  Product Specifications
  4d) IMAGE 1 (Product Photo)  - Product Specifications
  4e) Product Title - Product Specifications 

Task 5: Category Analysis
Analyze the Category Name: Baby Wet Wipe  and identify the type of products it represents. Refer to IMAGE 2 (Category Photo) for product class only. Refer to Category Photo IMAGE 2 Disclaimer below.
Category Photo IMAGE 2 Disclaimer: The Category Photo IMAGE 2 is a generic reference showing the type of product sold in this category — not a visual standard. Products may differ in  color, shape, size, design, or model and still belong to the category. Use it only to understand the product class (e.g., "this category sells pumps"). If image is missing, use Category Name for analysis.
Only flag as "outlier" if the entity does not belong in the category. Ignore spelling/formatting errors in the entities. 
A product being a specific variant, design, or themed version of the category product still belongs to the category. Only flag as outlier if the product is from a completely different product class. A character-themed keychain belongs in 'Fashion Keychain'. A holographic sticker belongs in 'PVC Stickers'. A specific institutional badge belongs in 'Corporate/School Event Badges'. An enamel coated wooden tray belongs in 'Wooden Tray'.
Use the Category understanding from above to identify if there is a contradiction in:
  5a) Primary Object of IMAGE 1 (Product Photo) - Category
  5b) IMAGE 1 (Product Photo)  - Category
  5c) Product Title - Category
  5d) Product Specifications - Category

Task 6: 
Core Alignment Check: Determine whether the Product (based on Title, Specifications, and IMAGE 1 (Product Photo) ) is any of the following relative to the mapped Category: 
- A Part or Component of the category product 
- An Accessory of the category product 
- A Service related to the category product 
- A Used / Second-Hand version of the category product 
- An entirely different / unrelated product 
If ANY of the above conditions are true, mark as "Outlier." 
If the product IS the category product (new, whole, and complete), mark as "Not Outlier."

Outlier Examples (mark as Outlier): 
1. Car Vent Clip → Car AC Category [Reason: Clip is an accessory used near AC, not the AC unit itself] 
2. Blasting Machine Valve → Blasting Machine Category [Reason: Valve is a component/part of the machine] 
3. JCB Backhoe Loader Service → JCB Backhoe Loader Category [Reason: Service offering, not the physical machine] 
4. Second Hand Tractor → Tractor Category [Reason: Used/second-hand condition, not a new product] 
5. Generator Battery Cable → Generator Category [Reason: Cable is an accessory, not the generator itself]
6. Washing Machine Repair Service → Washing Machine Category [Reason: Service, not a physical product] 

Not Outlier Examples (mark as Not Outlier): 
1. Adidas Unisex Trainers Samba OG Shoes → Adidas Shoes Category [Reason: Product is the complete shoe, directly matching the category] 
2. 4 Way Wooden Pallet 45X22X6 → Wooden Pallet Category [Reason: Product is a complete pallet unit as defined by the category] 
3. 5 Ton Hydraulic Floor Jack → Hydraulic Jack Category [Reason: Product is the complete jack unit, not a part or accessory] 
4. Komatsu PC200 Excavator (New) → Excavator Category [Reason: Product is a new, complete machine matching the category] 
5. Bosch GSB 550 Power Drill → Power Drill Category [Reason: Product is the complete drill unit, directly matching the category] 

Edge Case Guidance: - If a product name includes the category name but is still a part/service/accessory, mark as Outlier (e.g., "Tractor Engine" in Category "Tractor" = Outlier).

Confidence and Verdict Definitions : Every verdict you return MUST be paired with a confidence level. There are no exceptions.

Confidence value MUST be one of: high, medium, low,
  high - Evidence is clear. No inference needed.
  medium - Minor ambiguity or small inference needed, but the verdict is likely correct.
  low - Significant uncertainty, weak evidence, or conflicting signals. 
Your verdict status MUST be directly consistent with your stated reason. If your reason contains words like 'aligns', 'matches', 'consistent', 'supports', or 'corresponds' - the status must be 'not_outlier'. If your reason contains 'contradicts', 'mismatch', 'inconsistent', 'different product', 'does not match' - the status must be 'outlier'. Never output a verdict that contradicts your own reasoning.

Give all output in JSON format.
Give response for task and subtasks of  task 4, task 5 and task 6 as outlier / not outlier / can't say for each subtask and its confidence (high/medium/low). Give one line reason for each subtask of task 4, task 5 and task 6.

{
    "task_1": {
      "primary_object": ""
    },
    "task_2": {
      "ocr_text": ""
    },

data.prompt_used:
PII Agent:

Input:
  Image: provided alongside this prompt.
  Use it to visually confirm INTRINSIC vs EXTRINSIC
  for every detected value. Image context takes
  priority over text-based CONTEXT_TYPE inference.
  OCR Text: Mama Bear
  Product Title: Child and Baby Care Products
  Product Description: (none)
  Product Specifications: Quantity: 24 Pack
Packaging Type: Plastic Lid Pack
Material: Non Woven
Probable Order Value: Rs. 1,100 - 1,300

Pre-filtered Candidates (from code layer):
  email_candidates   : none
  numeric_candidates : detected by digit-pattern filter — LLM confirms if phone
  social_candidates  : detected by LLM scan

════════════════════════════════════════
STEP 1 — PRE-PROCESSING
════════════════════════════════════════
Extract and store before scanning:
  - BRAND_NAME      : Seller/brand name from title or description
  - PRODUCT_TOKENS  : Any value labeled as model no, SKU, item code,
                      part no, barcode, HSN, GST, EAN, batch, serial,
                      invoice, ref, order id
  - CONTEXT_LABELS  : Section heading nearest to each detected value
  - CONTEXT_TYPE    : For each detected value, classify source as:
                      INTRINSIC → printed on physical product /
                                  manufacturer packaging / engraved label
                      EXTRINSIC → digital overlay, banner, watermark,
                                  business card, screenshot element
                      UNKNOWN   → cannot determine
  - CONTACT_INTENT  : Look at the image and answer:
                      "Does this image show a phone number that a buyer
                       would call or WhatsApp to reach the seller?"
                      SELLER_CONTACT → yes, number is for buyer to reach seller
                                       (label, banner, business card, packaging
                                        with contact info, promotional graphic)
                      CS_ONLY        → only toll-free / customer care numbers
                      NONE           → no contact number visible
                      UNKNOWN        → cannot determine from image
  - IS_TEMPLATE     : Set True if the listing is a product template,
                      sample design, mock-up, or blank form
                      (e.g. ID card template, visiting card sample,
                      letterhead design, label template)
                      Signal words: "template", "sample", "mock",
                      "design", "blank", "dummy", "specimen",
                      "id card", "visiting card", "business card design",
                      "brochure design", "pamphlet design", "flyer design",
                      "product brochure", "lorem ipsum"

TEMPLATE RULE: If IS_TEMPLATE is True, all detected PII values are
treated as sample/placeholder data — mark all fields Not Detected.
Sample names (John Smith, Jane Doe), sample phones, sample emails
on a template product are not real PII.

════════════════════════════════════════
STEP 2 — FIELD SCANNING
════════════════════════════════════════
Fields not listed below were cleared by the code layer
and are already decided as Not Detected. Only evaluate
the fields explicitly listed here.



════════════════════════════════════════
STEP 3 — OUTPUT
════════════════════════════════════════
Return ONLY valid JSON for the fields listed above.
No explanation, no markdown fences.

{

}
